title: salix × glaucovillosa
intro: Salix × glaucovillosa, also known as Glaucous Willow, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Salix lucida (Shining Willow) and Salix lasiolepis (Arroyo Willow). The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix × glaucovillosa include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, as well as being used topically in salves and creams.
